added_assumptions.label: thm:surrogate-certificate:BudgetAdmissible
added_assumptions.statement: BudgetAdmissible ε B  (B ∈ [card I·ε, card I·(1-ε)] — feasible-budget nonemptiness regularity condition)
added_assumptions.source: F3 de-launder: dropped assumed selector-optimality binders hopt/hsurr; feasibleSet nonemptiness (from BudgetAdmissible) makes optimalDesign/surrogateDesign optimality DERIVABLE via compact attainment (reuse convex_design), so it is proven not assumed.

added_assumptions.label: thm:hetero-clt:denomKernelRegularity
added_assumptions.statement: Tendsto (denominatorKernelBound(ε n) dbar / card(Ox n)) atTop (𝓝 0) — uniform ε-floor / denominator-rate REGULARITY (design non-degeneracy across the triangular array)
added_assumptions.source: Found by de-gate: hetero_clt was UNDER-SPECIFIED (no asymptotic ε-floor). Added as a faithful non-degeneracy regularity condition; the linearization is now derivable.

added_assumptions.label: thm:hetero-clt:epsilonFloor
added_assumptions.statement: ∃ ε0>0, ∀ᶠ n, ε0 ≤ ε n — uniform ε-floor (triangular-array design non-degeneracy)
added_assumptions.source: 2nd under-specification found: engine needs a UNIFORM summand bound; added the uniform floor. Faithful non-degeneracy condition.

proposed_from.topic: Minimax-optimal heterogeneous Bernoulli design for bipartite-interference experiments: minimize a graph-only worst-case design-based variance envelope (the supremum of the Hajek estimator's asymptotic variance over bounded potential outcomes, a closed-form functional of the bipartite graph G and the per-intervention-unit probability vector p) over p subject to a budget and positivity floor, delivering the optimal design p*(G) with an OBSERVABLE optimality certificate --- a convex-relaxation-plus-rounding approximation-ratio bound alpha provably nonvacuous under an explicit intervention-unit output-degree-dispersion regime --- together with the supporting non-identical-Bernoulli Hajek martingale CLT and conservative variance estimator establishing the design's Wald-coverage validity; extends 'Design-based causal inference in bipartite experiments' (arXiv:2501.09844), which proves the CLT and closed-form variance only for a homogeneous Bernoulli(p) design and explicitly leaves heterogeneous per-unit probabilities to future research.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The nearest in-repo comparator, exp_design_pm1_exactness_boundary_v1, studies covariance-matrix implementability for a one-mode interference SDP. The present kernel instead chooses a heterogeneous Bernoulli probability vector in a bipartite design-based Hajek problem, so the decision variable, estimand, and certificate geometry differ materially.","published_axis":"The closest published anchor, LuShiFangZhangDing2025BipartiteDesign, proves only homogeneous Bernoulli(p) bipartite Hajek inference and explicitly leaves richer intervention strategies for future work. No adjacent paper supplies a graph-only worst-case Hajek envelope over heterogeneous p together with an optimality certificate and supporting design-based inference theory for the same design object."}
seed_list: Closed-form heterogeneous Hajek variance envelope and exact minimax design over p.
Observable surrogate design with nonvacuous approximation certificate under degree-dispersion.
Non-identical-Bernoulli bipartite Hajek CLT with explicit variance matrices.
Conservative post-design variance estimator and Wald coverage after graph-only design choice.
Integrated package: envelope + optimal design + heterogeneous CLT + conservative Wald + open certificate.
Degree-regular special-case exactness of the homogeneous design inside the heterogeneous class.
Estimator-class optimal conservative variance within quadratic completions for heterogeneous bipartite Hajek.
Relaxation-loss frontier for graph-only surrogate design under output-degree imbalance.

seed_details.seed: Closed-form heterogeneous Hajek variance envelope and exact minimax design over p.
seed_details.red_team: Immediate from Lu et al. because heterogeneous Bernoulli only changes p^{|N_i|} to products of p_k.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the Hajek variance and its worst-case envelope become pair-specific reciprocal-product loads coupled by the global budget and positivity constraints.

seed_details.seed: Observable surrogate design with nonvacuous approximation certificate under degree-dispersion.
seed_details.red_team: Looks like a routine relaxation-and-rounding certificate transplanted from prior SDP design work.
seed_details.rebuttal: Not routine: the feasible set is continuous Bernoulli probabilities and the objective is a reciprocal-product Hajek envelope, so the observable certificate must be derived from new graph-only surrogate loads rather than covariance implementability geometry.

seed_details.seed: Non-identical-Bernoulli bipartite Hajek CLT with explicit variance matrices.
seed_details.red_team: Too close to Leung plus Lu: just route new exposure bookkeeping into an existing dependency CLT.
seed_details.rebuttal: The product-exposure/Hajek denominator algebra is new, but by itself the limit law risks the same downgrade as prior in-repo exposure-CLT work.

seed_details.seed: Conservative post-design variance estimator and Wald coverage after graph-only design choice.
seed_details.red_team: Just port Lu et al.'s conservative variance proof to p_k and condition on the chosen design.
seed_details.rebuttal: The heterogeneous design pushes inclusion probabilities toward the floor and changes the unobservable cross-arm block; the post-selection conservativeness statement is not automatic.

seed_details.seed: Integrated package: envelope + optimal design + heterogeneous CLT + conservative Wald + open certificate.
seed_details.anchor: LuShiFangZhangDing2025BipartiteDesign open problems 1-4, with exactness-boundary prior proposal as certificate template
seed_details.red_team: Overpacked: probably four papers masquerading as one proposal.
seed_details.rebuttal: The pieces are technically coupled by one decision object p and one design-based estimator; the CLT and Wald theory are supporting theorems required to make the optimal design operational.

seed_details.seed: Degree-regular special-case exactness of the homogeneous design inside the heterogeneous class.
seed_details.red_team: A regular-graph exactness characterization is a corollary once the full convex program is solved.
seed_details.rebuttal: The objection holds; this is a reduction, not a headline kernel.

seed_details.seed: Estimator-class optimal conservative variance within quadratic completions for heterogeneous bipartite Hajek.
seed_details.red_team: HMS already own the estimator-class optimization problem; this would be a special case.
seed_details.rebuttal: The objection substantially holds once the class is framed too broadly; kept only as inspiration for the post-design variance statement, not as the kernel.

seed_details.seed: Relaxation-loss frontier for graph-only surrogate design under output-degree imbalance.
seed_details.red_team: Without the exact envelope theorem this is comparing a surrogate to an unknown target and risks becoming a vacuous graph heuristic.
seed_details.rebuttal: The objection holds at cold start; it only becomes meaningful as an open-ended question inside the integrated package.

proposed_from.literature_map: Lu et al. (2025) provide the direct homogeneous-Bernoulli bipartite Hajek framework and explicitly leave richer assignment mechanisms open. Harshaw et al. (2021) provide the closest bipartite design-aware algebra but under a linear exposure-response model. Viviano (2026) is the closest design-for-precision analogue, but it is pilot-based and one-mode. Leung (2019/2021) shows non-identical Bernoulli assignment can coexist with network CLTs, though not for bipartite all-treated/all-control product exposures. Aronow-Samii (2017) provide the exposure-probability and conservative-variance calculus. Ugander et al. (2013) and Ugander-Yin (2020) motivate graph-regime certificates. Park-Wager (2026) and Harshaw-Middleton-Savje (2021/2024) shape the conservative-variance layer but do not solve the heterogeneous bipartite design problem.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: headline / thm:convex-design
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The optimality claim is envelope-optimal for the graph-only conservative objective V_env, not exact minimax optimality for the true design variance over bounded potential outcomes.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The design contribution is a conservative-envelope optimal heterogeneous Bernoulli design under the stated budget and positivity constraints.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:hetero-clt / thm:postdesign-wald
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The inference results are conditional on bounded overlap-dependency and nondegenerate asymptotic variance at the selected p_n^*(G_n).
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Coverage is pointwise along graph/potential-outcome sequences satisfying those primitive regularity and nondegeneracy conditions.

banked_reason: Verified CKPT2: the migrated dispersion counterexample theorem is fully proved, dual-model matched, unconditional over its stated parameter domains, and axiom-clean under the project convention.
